# Description

This is a stereoscopic card (stereograph) featuring two nearly identical sepia-toned photographs side by side, numbered 58 and numbered 21084. The image shows a woman in early 20th-century clothing—including a long dark dress, white blouse, and hat—holding what appears to be ice cream on a utensil or small tool, offering it to a small black bear cub standing on its hind legs. The scene takes place outdoors in what appears to be an enclosure with a wire or mesh fence visible in the background, alongside stone or brick structures and vegetation. The left edge contains text reading "Keystone View Company" and "Stereographs Colorakers" vertically, while the right edge reads "Keystone View Card, Chicago, Ill. London, England." At the bottom of the card, caption text reads "Mrs. Herbert Hoover Feeds Ice Cream to a Small Black Bear Cub, Seward, Alaska." A grayscale reference chart is visible at the top of the image.

This black and white photograph shows an urban landscape of devastation and reconstruction. In the foreground, extensive rubble and debris are scattered across the street, including broken brick, stone, and metal fragments. On the left side stands a large neoclassical building with a prominent domed roof featuring a geometric lattice pattern, supported by tall white columns with Corinthian capitals. The building appears largely intact despite surrounding destruction. Overhead, multiple electrical wires and trolley lines crisscross the sky. In the middle distance, a street recedes with a few pedestrians and horse-drawn vehicles visible. On the right side, severely damaged brick buildings with partial walls and skeletal wooden framework remain standing, showing extreme structural destruction. The street surface in the foreground features a pattern of lines, possibly indicating trolley tracks or worn paving. # Description

This vintage postcard features Sdreckels Rotisserie, located on the 15th Floor of the Call Building in San Francisco, with proprietor A. Wolff. The composition is divided into three sections: On the left is an illustration of a tall ornate building with a distinctive tower and cupola labeled "CAFE." The central and largest section depicts the interior of the establishment, showing two elegantly dressed diners seated at a table with wine bottles, glasses, and dining items. Through large windows behind them, an expansive cityscape view of San Francisco's hillside buildings and structures is visible. At the bottom of this central scene, text reads "ICE CREAM PARLOR · CAFE AND RESTAURANT 250 FEET ABOVE THE BAY." An ornate brown and gold banner with curved decorative elements at the top displays the establishment's name. In the upper right corner, publisher information reads "EDWARD H. MITCHELL, PUBLISHER; SAN FRANCISCO." The postcard uses soft colors including light blue, cream, brown, and muted tones throughout the hand-colored lithographic design.
Collection name: Walter Robert and Gail Lynn Kransky collection of Edward H. Mitchell postcards
